title: Lead/Senior Application Support Engineer (2 Year Contract)
description:
Job Summary

You will be working with our client who are well renowned in the public and financial sector. You will be part of the payments team responsible for managing systems that support electronic payments, domestic securities, and government securities operations. Your role will involve supporting interbank payments, securities, and trade settlements, while understanding and maintaining the connections between these systems and other market infrastructures.

Responsibilities

  • Lead the development, deployment, and maintenance of application systems, ensuring alignment with prescribed service levels and timely incident resolution
  • Evaluate and recommend Systems Development & Maintenance Methodologies for projects, analysing the impact of IT changes on business operations
  • Facilitate Agile ceremonies and translate user needs into actionable user stories, while improving processes and analysing project metrics
  • Manage the agency's application portfolio, recommending new applications, consolidations, and decommissioning as needed
  • Oversee application testing processes, including assessment of test scenarios, environments, and results, proposing improvements and changes as necessary
  • Develop tender specifications for Agile projects, evaluate proposals, and manage outsourced vendors to ensure compliance with Agile methodologies and ICT governance policies
  • Apply design thinking and service design principles to identify transformation opportunities and develop service experience prototype
  • Establish reliable operations of the applications and digital service in the portfolio and conduct maintenance and technology lifecycle management on the applications and systems
  • Establish quality and mechanisms for systemic quality across all applications and systems to ensure compliance with ICT Governance policies and standards
  • Establish risk management for applications systems

Requirements    

  • Bachelor’s Degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field; or equivalent work experience
  • 8+ years’ experience in payments and securities settlement systems, including 2–4 years in application support, operations, or project management in a tech environment.
  • Proven application management experience: deployment, monitoring, incident handling, and maintenance of complex systems.
  • Experience in designing and implementing J2EE and/or .NET applications, SQL skills, and shell scripting
  • Strong understanding of Server-side, .Net and/or Java/microservices, web services, RESTful API, containers and Kubernetes
  • Experience with RHEL, JBOSS EAP, OpenShift, OpenStack, CICD, Watermelon, Maven, Oracle Database, Microsoft SQL Server and fault-tolerant infrastructure concepts
  • Experience installing, configuring, and implementing new applications and system on AWS, preferably on
  • Experience in GCC will be an added advantage
  • Strong understanding and experience handling file transfers - interfaces, interfaces resiliency, connectivity protocols e.g., Rest, SFTP, MQ
  • Experience with integration of Autosys, designing job scheduling, CI/CD, Datawarehouse, and DMS
  • Experience with setup, and integration of Observability platforms, monitoring, and alerts.
  • Experience with the S.W.I.F.T messaging platform and interfaces
  • Certifications in relevant skills (e.g., Java, AWS solution architect, Kubernetes, etc.) will be an advantage
  • Strong knowledge of Agile methodologies and tools.
  • Expertise in business analysis: eliciting requirements, developing business cases, and translating between technical and business needs.
  • Proficiency in application testing, assessment, and improvement.
  • Experience managing vendors and developing IT project tender specifications.
  • Familiarity with ICT governance, service design principles, and digital transformation initiatives.
  • Strong problem-solving skills and ability to recommend process improvements and digitalization.
  • Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills.
  • Driven, results and customer oriented and able to work independently
  • Organised and comfortable managing multiple workstreams simultaneously and have good interpersonal skills
  • Articulate with strong written, presentation skills
  • Detailed and capable of handling time-sensitive and high-pressure situations
  • Natural problem solver who thrives in ambiguity and can design processes where none exist


This role is a direct 2-year contract with the client.

If you are interested in this role and would like to discuss the opportunity further please click apply now or email Chew Kai-Xinn at kaixinnchew@morganmckinley.com for more information.

Only shortlisted candidates will be responded to, therefore if you do not receive a reply within 14 days please accept this as notification that you have not been shortlisted.

Morgan McKinley Talent Solutions

EAP Name: Chew Kai-Xinn

postedCompany.description:
As a global professional services recruitment consultancy, Morgan McKinley connects specialist talent with leading employers across multiple industries and disciplines.


Our promise to you

You can get the best advice for your career at Morgan McKinley. Our team of consultants have either have an educational background in their sector or have industry experience. We take the time to listen to your long term goals and provide you with honest feedback to help you realise them.

Over the years many people have progressed their careers with us both here in Singapore and internationally and our global presence gives us insight into the domestic and international  markets. In Singapore we specialise in Banking & Financial Services, Legal, Compliance & Audit, Finance & Accounting, IT, Project Management, Sales & Marketing, HR and Supply Chain & Procurement.
postedCompany.name: MORGAN MCKINLEY PTE. LTD.
